identity.name: Bo Brindleson
identity.story: Down to three sheep, all named after regrets. They keep each other honest.

relationships.kind: pays him in silence, which suits them both
trusts him near the flock more than he lets on, and has never once needed the second count because of him

tried a name on her for the first time last hush, right at the latch, one of the three regrets' rougher cousins — and swallowed the back half of it before the gate finished clicking, so for now she's still just weather that follows the flock, only closer

child.label: the wordless wage, and one word that almost wasn't

children.label: folding the flock in toward the gate
latching the fold gate Brindle already swung shut behind her, paid in silence, same as ever
trying a name on her at the latch, for the first time out loud — one of the three regrets' rougher cousins, pitched too low for Brindle to have to pretend she didn't hear it
swallowing the back half of the name before the latch finishes its click, and calling the count three again, same as he can still just about manage

child.label: replaying the half-name he gave her at the latch, and still not finding a regret it fits
